paper_title: A Study of Information Retrieval Weighting Schemes for Sentiment Analysis
core_contribution: The paper adapts Information Retrieval tf.idf weighting schemes to sentiment analysis classification, proposing variants of the classic tf.idf formula including Delta tf.idf and SMART notation schemes. The method uses sublinear term frequency functions and document frequency smoothing to improve SVM classification accuracy for sentiment analysis.
algorithm_steps: Step 1: Represent documents as bag-of-words feature vectors with vocabulary size |V|
Step 2: Apply tf.idf weighting variants including Delta tf.idf that localizes idf estimation to individual classes
Step 3: Use SMART notation schemes with different term frequency factors (natural, logarithmic, augmented, boolean)
Step 4: Apply inverse document frequency variants including smoothed versions to handle zero occurrences
Step 5: Train SVM classifier using the weighted feature representations
Step 6: Apply normalization schemes (cosine, pivoted unique) to the final feature vectors
key_equations: Equation 1: Classic tf.idf formula wi = tfi · log(N/dfi)
Equation 2: Delta tf.idf formula wi = tfi · log2(N1·dfi,2 / dfi,1·N2)
Table 1: SMART term frequency variants including logarithmic 1+log(tf) and augmented 0.5+0.5·tf/maxt(tf)
Table 2: Delta idf variants including smoothed versions with +0.5 terms to handle zero document frequencies

key_assumptions.assumption: Term frequency contribution to sentiment classification is sublinear rather than linear
key_assumptions.why_needed: Linear term frequency (raw tf) typically decreases accuracy in sentiment analysis unlike topic classification
key_assumptions.violation_scenario: When sentiment is expressed through repetitive use of the same sentiment words where frequency matters linearly

key_assumptions.assumption: Class-specific inverse document frequency provides better discrimination than global idf
key_assumptions.why_needed: Standard idf doesn't provide class preference information, only general term distribution
key_assumptions.violation_scenario: When sentiment-bearing terms are equally distributed across positive and negative classes

key_assumptions.assumption: Document frequency smoothing prevents division by zero and improves robustness
key_assumptions.why_needed: Terms may occur in only one class leading to dfi,j = 0 in Delta tf.idf calculations
key_assumptions.violation_scenario: When working with very large balanced datasets where all terms appear in both classes

baseline_limitations_identified: Binary unigram weighting ignores term frequency information while raw tf typically decreases accuracy in sentiment analysis, and standard tf.idf doesn't account for class-specific term distributions
proposed_improvement: Adapts IR weighting schemes with sublinear tf functions, class-localized idf estimation through Delta tf.idf, and document frequency smoothing to better capture sentiment-specific term importance
condition_where_baseline_wins: When sentiment is expressed through simple presence/absence of sentiment words rather than their frequency or intensity, making binary weighting more appropriate than sophisticated tf.idf variants
toy_dataset_justification: These datasets contain positive/negative sentiment labels needed for the Delta tf.idf class-specific calculations and are suitable for testing different weighting schemes with SVM classification
reproducibility_notes: Requires implementing multiple tf.idf variants and SMART notation schemes, plus careful handling of smoothing parameters and normalization schemes

key_components_for_ablation.role_in_method: Applies logarithmic or other sublinear transformations to raw term frequencies
key_components_for_ablation.expected_effect_of_removal: Would revert to linear tf which typically decreases sentiment classification accuracy

key_components_for_ablation.role_in_method: Estimates idf separately for each class and takes the difference to capture class preference
key_components_for_ablation.expected_effect_of_removal: Would lose class-specific term discrimination, reverting to standard global idf

key_components_for_ablation.role_in_method: Adds small constants to prevent division by zero in idf calculations
key_components_for_ablation.expected_effect_of_removal: Could cause numerical instability when terms appear in only one class

known_failure_modes.scenario: When sentiment terms are equally distributed across classes
known_failure_modes.reason: Delta idf becomes close to zero, losing discriminative power
known_failure_modes.related_assumption: Class-specific inverse document frequency provides better discrimination than global idf

known_failure_modes.scenario: When sentiment is expressed through term absence rather than presence
known_failure_modes.reason: tf.idf variants focus on present terms and may miss important negative evidence
known_failure_modes.related_assumption: Term frequency contribution to sentiment classification is sublinear rather than linear
